description: MCP server that gives any AI coding tool a structured second opinion from another AI provider

stories.title: Project restructure + Zod response schemas
stories.description: Delete old src/index.ts. Create new directory structure: src/providers/, src/tools/. Create src/schemas.ts with all Zod output schemas for the 4 tools (AskResponse, ReviewResponse, VerifyResponse, CompareResponse). Create src/types.ts with the Provider interface. All schemas must match the exact JSON output format defined in CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: src/schemas.ts exists with 4 exported Zod schemas
npm run build passes (may have unused exports)

stories.title: Structured output pipeline
stories.description: Create src/structured-output.ts. Implement the JSON parse pipeline: (1) try JSON.parse on raw response, (2) if fails, retry with correction prompt, (3) if fails, regex extract between { and }, (4) validate with Zod schema. Export getStructuredResponse() that takes raw string + ZodSchema and returns validated object or error response.
acceptance_criteria: Handles: valid JSON, malformed JSON (retry), no JSON (regex), invalid schema (Zod error)
Returns structured error object on all failure paths, never throws

stories.title: Gemini provider
stories.description: Create src/providers/gemini.ts. Implement the Provider interface using @google/genai SDK. Must support: system instruction, temperature, maxOutputTokens, 30s timeout via Promise.race. Use Context7 to verify the current @google/genai API before coding.
acceptance_criteria: src/providers/gemini.ts exists and implements Provider interface
Error handling returns formatted error, never throws unhandled

stories.title: OpenAI-compatible provider
stories.description: Create src/providers/openai-compatible.ts. Implement the Provider interface using the openai npm package. Must work with OpenAI, Groq, and DeepSeek by accepting a configurable baseURL. Support json_mode via response_format: { type: 'json_object' }. 30s timeout. Install openai package.
acceptance_criteria: src/providers/openai-compatible.ts exists and implements Provider interface

stories.title: Provider registry + config
stories.description: Create src/providers/registry.ts and src/config.ts. Registry auto-detects which provider to use by scanning env vars in priority order: SECOND_OPINION_PROVIDER override > GEMINI_API_KEY > OPENAI_API_KEY > GROQ_API_KEY > DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Config exports provider map with env_key, models, default model, and baseURL for each. SECOND_OPINION_MODEL overrides default model.
acceptance_criteria: src/providers/registry.ts exports getProvider() that returns instantiated provider
src/config.ts exports PROVIDER_CONFIG with all provider definitions
Auto-detection works: finds first available key
Clear error message if no provider key found

stories.title: System prompts for all 4 tools
stories.description: Create src/prompts.ts. Each prompt: (1) defines the AI's role, (2) specifies the EXACT JSON schema it must return, (3) includes examples of valid output, (4) says 'respond in pt-BR', (5) says 'return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences'. Prompts for: ask (verdict+confidence+answer+evidence), review (verdict+score+criteria), verify (verdict+confidence+explanation+caveats), compare (winner+confidence+comparison+recommendation).
acceptance_criteria: src/prompts.ts exports 4 prompt functions (one per tool)
Each prompt includes the exact JSON schema in the instructions
Each prompt explicitly forbids markdown fences around JSON

stories.title: Tools: second_opinion_ask + second_opinion_verify
stories.description: Create src/tools/ask.ts and src/tools/verify.ts. Each tool: gets provider from registry, builds prompt, calls provider, parses structured output with Zod schema, returns validated JSON. ask: question+context -> verdict/confidence/answer/evidence. verify: claim+context -> verdict/confidence/explanation/caveats/sources_suggested (sources are doc names not URLs).
acceptance_criteria: Both use getStructuredResponse() for JSON validation
Both return provider and model in response

stories.title: Tools: second_opinion_review + second_opinion_compare
stories.description: Create src/tools/review.ts and src/tools/compare.ts. review: code+language+focus -> verdict(PASS/FAIL/WARNING)+score(1-10)+criteria[]+summary. Default focus: ['security','performance','correctness','error-handling']. compare: approach_a{name,description}+approach_b{name,description}+criteria[]+context -> winner+confidence+comparison[]+recommendation.
acceptance_criteria: review has default criteria when focus not provided
compare accepts approach objects with name+description

stories.title: MCP server entry point + wiring
stories.description: Create new src/index.ts. Shebang line. Import dotenv. Validate at least one provider key exists (clear error if not). Create McpServer. Register all 4 tools. StdioServerTransport. main().catch() with stderr error and exit(1).
acceptance_criteria: Registers 4 tools: second_opinion_ask, second_opinion_review, second_opinion_verify, second_opinion_compare
node dist/index.js without any key shows clear error and exits with code 1

stories.title: Package.json + .env.example + README
stories.description: Update package.json: name to second-opinion-mcp, add openai dependency, update bin entry. Create .env.example with ALL provider keys commented with instructions. Write professional README.md: what it is, why it exists (structured verdicts not chat), provider table, installation (one-liner for each provider), all 4 tools with input/output examples, free tier limits per provider.
acceptance_criteria: .env.example lists all provider keys with comments
README.md has: description, provider comparison table, installation commands, 4 tool examples with JSON output, limitations
